title: The Benefits of Regular Exercise
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Scientists (0) generally agree that regular physical activity is one of the most effective ways to improve health. Many adults, however, are completely (17) inactive and spend most of their day sitting down. Even moderate exercise can lead to clear (18) improvement in both energy levels and mood. People who work out regularly tend to develop greater physical (19) strength and are less likely to fall ill. Activities such as walking or swimming are highly (20) beneficial because they require little or no equipment. The main (21) difficulty for most people is finding the motivation to start and stick to a routine. Some people feel (22) uncomfortable exercising in public, which is why home workouts have grown in popularity. Health experts (23) regularly advise at least 150 minutes of moderate activity per week. Those who ignore this advice risk leading an (24) unhealthy lifestyle with serious long-term consequences.

example.text: Scientists (0) GENERALLY agree that regular physical activity is one of the most effective ways to improve health. (GENERAL)

questions.explanation: A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'in-' is added to 'active' to mean 'not exercising'.
A noun is needed after 'clear'; 'improve' becomes 'improvement' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed after 'physical'; the adjective 'strong' becomes the noun 'strength' through an irregular change.
An adjective is needed after 'highly'; 'benefit' becomes 'beneficial' by adding the suffix '-ial'.
A noun is needed as the subject; the adjective 'difficult' becomes the noun 'difficulty' by changing '-t' to '-ty'.
An adjective is needed after 'feel'; the prefix 'un-' and the suffix '-able' are added to 'comfort' to form a negative adjective.
An adverb is needed to modify the verb 'advise'; 'regular' becomes 'regularly' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
An adjective is needed before 'lifestyle'; 'health' becomes 'unhealthy' with the prefix 'un-' and suffix '-y'.
